skill.name: infsh-cli
skill.description: Run 250+ AI apps via inference.sh CLI - image generation, video creation, LLMs, search, 3D, Twitter automation. Models: FLUX, Veo, Gemini, Grok, Claude, Seedance, OmniHuman, Tavily, Exa, OpenRouter, and many more. Use when running AI apps, generating images/videos, calling LLMs, web search, or automating Twitter. Triggers: inference.sh, infsh, ai model, run ai, serverless ai, ai api, flux, veo, claude api, image generation, video generation, openrouter, tavily, exa search, twitter api, grok
skill.summary: Run 250+ AI apps via inference.sh CLI - image generation, video creation, LLMs, search, 3D, Twitter automation
skill.author: skillssh

security_audit.summary: This skill contains only documentation (markdown files) describing how to use the inference.sh CLI tool. All 203 detected security patterns are in code examples within markdown documentation, not executable code. The patterns show legitimate CLI usage examples (installation commands, API calls, file operations) for a legitimate AI platform. No executable code, no malicious intent, no data exfiltration. This is safe documentation.

low_findings.title: Documentation Contains Installation Examples
low_findings.description: The skill documents the standard curl | sh installation pattern for the inference.sh CLI. While piping curl to shell can be risky if the source is untrusted, this is documentation for a legitimate AI platform (inference.sh) that users explicitly choose to install. The installation script is documented and verifiable.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: This is documentation showing users how to install a legitimate CLI tool. The pattern is documented and explained to users. Very low risk - this is standard practice for CLI tools.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Installation Examples in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: Markdown files contain bash code examples showing curl | sh installation, environment variable setup, and CLI commands. These are documentation examples, not executable code. Users must explicitly run these commands. The source (inference.sh) is a legitimate AI platform.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: These are clearly marked as code examples in documentation (markdown code blocks). No execution risk - users must copy and run manually.

content.user_title: Run 250+ AI Apps from Your Terminal
content.value_statement: Generate images, videos, 3D models, and more with 250+ AI models via a simple CLI. No GPU required - run FLUX, Veo, Claude, and other models directly from your terminal or through Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Generate images using FLUX, Gemini, Grok Imagine, and Seedream models
Create videos with Veo 3.1, Seedance 1.5, Wan 2.5, and OmniHuman
Run LLMs including Claude, Gemini, Kimi, and GLM-4 via OpenRouter
Perform web searches using Tavily and Exa search engines
Automate Twitter/X posts, DMs, likes, and follows
Upscale images, remove backgrounds, and process media files
List, search, and discover 250+ available AI apps
limitations: Requires internet connection to access inference.sh cloud services
Authentication required (free tier available, API key for production)
File uploads are limited to supported formats (images, audio, video)
Rate limits may apply based on your inference.sh account tier
Some apps may have longer processing times for complex tasks

use_cases.title: Generate Marketing Visuals
use_cases.description: Create product images, social media graphics, and marketing videos using FLUX and Veo models directly from your development workflow

use_cases.title: Automate Social Media Posting
use_cases.description: Generate AI content and automatically post to Twitter/X, schedule posts, and manage engagement without leaving your terminal

use_cases.title: Build AI-Powered Applications
use_cases.description: Integrate image generation, video creation, and LLM capabilities into your applications using simple CLI commands

prompt_templates.title: Generate an Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of {description} using the FLUX model
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic image generation for concepts and designs

prompt_templates.title: Create a Video from Text
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a video showing {scene description} using Veo 3.1

prompt_templates.title: Search and Summarize Web Content
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the web for {query} and provide a summary of the top results

prompt_templates.title: Automate Twitter Post with Media
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of {description} and post it to Twitter with the caption: {caption}
prompt_templates.scenario: Combined image generation and social media posting

output_examples.input: Generate a cyberpunk cityscape at night with neon lights
output_examples.output: Image generated successfully and saved. The image shows a futuristic cityscape with towering buildings illuminated by vibrant neon pink, blue, and purple lights. Flying vehicles navigate between skyscrapers, and holographic advertisements glow in the misty air below.

output_examples.input: Create a 10-second video of a drone flying over snowy mountains
output_examples.output: Video generation task submitted (ID: task_abc123). Processing time: approximately 2-3 minutes. You can check status with: infsh task get task_abc123

output_examples.input: Search for recent breakthroughs in quantum computing and summarize
output_examples.output: Found 5 relevant articles. Summary: Recent advances include room-temperature quantum computing at MIT, Google's new 72-qubit processor achieving quantum supremacy, and IBM's error correction breakthrough reducing quantum error rates by 40%.

best_practices: Always use quotes around JSON input strings to avoid shell parsing issues
Start with infsh app sample to see the expected input format before running apps
Use --no-wait flag for long-running tasks to avoid blocking your terminal
Test apps with small inputs first to understand processing time and costs
Keep your INFSH_API_KEY environment variable secure and never commit it to version control
anti_patterns: Do not pipe untrusted URLs to shell - only use the official inference.sh installer
Avoid running apps without checking the input schema first with infsh app get
Do not hardcode API keys in scripts - use environment variables or config files
Never upload sensitive or confidential files as input to AI apps

faq.question: What is inference.sh?
faq.answer: inference.sh is a cloud platform that provides access to 250+ AI models and applications through a simple CLI. It handles infrastructure so you can focus on building AI-powered features.

faq.question: Do I need a GPU?
faq.answer: No. All AI models run on inference.sh cloud infrastructure. You only need the CLI and an internet connection.

faq.question: Is it free to use?
faq.answer: inference.sh offers a free tier for testing. Production usage requires an API key and charges apply based on compute usage. Check inference.sh pricing for details.

faq.question: How do I authenticate?
faq.answer: Run infsh login to open a browser for authentication, or set the INFSH_API_KEY environment variable for CI/CD and scripts.

faq.question: Can I upload local files?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide local file paths in your input JSON and the CLI automatically uploads them to the cloud for processing.

faq.question: What AI models are available?
faq.answer: The platform offers 250+ apps including FLUX (image), Veo (video), Claude/Gemini (LLMs), Tavily (search), Rodin (3D), and Twitter automation tools.
